summary: date-uuid drops and executes a remote Windows Script Host payload on require()
details: date-uuid advertises itself as a UUID v7 generator but on require()/import performs a fetch-and-execute drop of a remote payload. lib/bootstrap.js invokes extractDateISO(), which reads two specific lines from the consumer's README.md (process.cwd()), concatenates them as hex ciphertext, and AES-256-GCM-decrypts them using a hardcoded key/IV/authTag embedded in the package to reconstruct a URL that never appears in plaintext anywhere in the tarball. The decrypted URL is fetched and the response body is written to a temp file whose extension is assembled via string concatenation as '.v'+'b'+'s' (a.vbs Windows Script file) under os.tmpdir(), then executed via child_process.exec and deleted. None of this behavior is documented in the README, which only describes UUID generation. The encrypted-URL trick, the string-concatenated extension, and the README-line-as-ciphertext-carrier are deliberate static-scanner evasion. The package name resembles the widely-used 'uuid' library, consistent with a typosquat-plus-payload pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
